mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI inference operator designed for serving machine learning models on Kubernetes. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of LLMs, embeddings, and speech-to-text models, providing intelligent scaling and optimized routing. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the need for specific configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Helm repository for KubeAI
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeAI Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs KubeAI in the 'kubeai' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kubeai

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

```

> Creates the namespace for KubeAI.
> Installs KubeAI with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeAI pods are running successfully in the kubeai namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Lists all pods in the kubeai namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource for KubeAI to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  modelPath: /path/to/model
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace '/path/to/model' with the actual path to your model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  modelPath: /path/to/model
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is ready for use.

```bash

kubectl get model example-model -n kubeai

```

> Checks the status of the example model.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all models and configurations associated with KubeAI.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls KubeAI from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the kubeai namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI to a new version
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your existing configurations. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-models-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backs up existing models to a YAML file.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-models-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image is not available or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' for details and adjust configurations accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Custom Resource does not exist or was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Custom Resource definition is correct and reapply it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the KubeAI pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits for the KubeAI deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running successfully with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai' showing 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
